definitions.definition: The limit of a function f(x) as x approaches a is denoted by f(x) and is defined as the value which the function approaches as the independent variable x gets arbitrarily close to a. In other words, it is the value that the function tends to, as x tends to a.
The left-hand limit of a function f(x) as x approaches a is denoted by lim x→a- f(x) and is defined as the value which the function approaches from the left side, as x gets arbitrarily close to a.
The right-hand limit of a function f(x) as x approaches a is denoted by lim x→a+ f(x) and is defined as the value which the function approaches from the right side, as x gets arbitrarily close to a.
The limit of a function f(x) at a point a is denoted by f(a) and is defined as the value which the function approaches as x gets arbitrarily close to a.
The limit of a sequence {an} is denoted by lim n→∞ an and is defined as the value which the sequence approaches as n gets arbitrarily large.
The limit of a series ∑an is denoted by lim n→∞ ∑an and is defined as the value which the series approaches as n gets arbitrarily large.
The derivative of a function f(x) at a point a is denoted by f'(a) and is defined as the rate of change of the function with respect to x at that point.
The derivative of a power function f(x) = x^n is denoted by f'(x) = nx^(n-1).
The derivative of a polynomial function f(x) = a_n x^n + a_(n-1) x^(n-1) + ... + a_1 x + a_0 is denoted by f'(x) = n a_n x^(n-1) + (n-1) a_(n-1) x^(n-2) + ... + a_1.

definitions.example: If f(x) = x^2 and g(x) = 3x, then f(x)g'(x) + f'(x)g(x) = x^2 (6x) + 2x(3x)
definitions.definition: The product rule states that if f(x) and g(x) are two functions, then the derivative of their product is f(x)g'(x) + f'(x)g(x).
The quotient rule states that if f(x) and g(x) are two functions, then the derivative of their quotient is (f(x)g'(x) - f'(x)g(x)) / g(x)^2.

definitions.example: If f(x) = sin(x^2), then f'(x) = cos(x^2) * 2x
definitions.definition: The chain rule states that if f(x) is a composite function of the form f(g(x)), then the derivative of f(x) is f'(g(x)) * g'(x).

definitions.example: If f(x) = x^3, then f'(x) = 3x^2 and f''(x) = 6x
definitions.definition: Higher-order derivatives are derivatives of derivatives. For example, the second derivative of a function f(x) is denoted by f''(x) and is defined as the derivative of f'(x).
The derivative of a trigonometric function f(x) = sin(x) is denoted by f'(x) = cos(x).
The derivative of an exponential function f(x) = a^x is denoted by f'(x) = a^x * ln(a).
The geometric interpretation of limits states that the limit of a function f(x) as x approaches a is the value which the graph of the function approaches as x gets arbitrarily close to a.
The squeeze theorem states that if a function f(x) is sandwiched between two functions g(x) and h(x) such that lim x→a g(x) = lim x→a h(x) = k, then lim x→a f(x) = k.
